title: Isolated 3D Two-Band Crossings Are Weyl Nodes
summary: A generic isolated two-band crossing in three dimensions linearizes to a Weyl Hamiltonian, with chirality given by the sign of the Jacobian determinant.
assumptions: The crossing is simple and isolated.
A local two-band reduction is valid.
scope: Lecture One codimension-counting and linearization result.

source_anchors.notes: The local Hamiltonian is identified with a Weyl Hamiltonian.

representation: Genericity claim about isolated 3D two-band crossings.
failure_modes: If the determinant vanishes or additional symmetries impose higher-order touching, the simple Weyl story can fail.
retrieval_hints: Use for the local identification of a 3D crossing as a Weyl point.
